Transcrição Completa: 7 Trading Prop Firm Secrets
[00:00]
"Alright. So, thank you, Jeremy. Appreciate you like always. Thank you for having me over here. So, yeah, pretty much I'm a trader. I started my career as a trader, just trading."
[00:15]
"One of my good friends was a marketer. I'm like, some of you guys here as well. And he was my neighbor. I had a Lambo. He had a 720. He always wondered what I did. I always wondered what he did. You know, like the guy on the block with the nice cars that you're like, what the fuck does this guy do, right?"
[00:35]
"And then we connected through the car wash guy. He's like, 'Man, you're crushing it with trading. Why don't you get into courses?' And that's kind of how I got into courses, trading, and eventually now we have a prop firm, right?"
[00:50]
"How many of you guys here use a prop firm? You guys use prop firms? Okay. How many of you guys know how a prop firm works? Like truly works, right? So that's why I'm here."
[01:05]
"One thing about me like as a trader I've always been transparent in my whole career as a trader. Because how many of you guys here are entrepreneurs? Everybody, right? Everybody here's an entrepreneur."
[01:20]
"So a trader and an entrepreneur is really the same thing because as an entrepreneur, you know, people like, 'Oh, I don't want to get into trading. It sounds a little risky.' You're going to risk something in life at some point. You're always going to have risk in life."
[01:35]
"It doesn't matter. You not leaving your house is a risk because you might sit too long and die from a blood clot. You leaving your house, you might get shot. Everything in life is a risk, right? It's just taking the correct risks for you guys to make money."
[01:50]
"So, a lot of people always like, 'Oh, I don't know if trading is for me.' Listen, I'm not a trader. I sit right here like, I trade to make money, but I also have a trucking company with 150 trucks. I also do real estate. I also do this."
[02:10]
"I don't really consider myself a trader even though I make most of my money and income from trading, right? Because that's like the easiest thing for me. I'll make 70 grand in a day, 100 grand in a day. I've made my biggest day trading has been half a mill in a day."
[02:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Risk vs Reward in Trading"
NÚMEROS: $500,000 ganho (maior dia), $700,000 perda (maior dia)
VISUAL: Comparação de ganhos e perdas, conceito de risco bilateral, mentalidade de trader
[02:35]
"But obviously there's a risk side, right, that nobody talks about. My biggest loss in a day has been 700 grand. I almost didn't speak for like three days after that, right?"
[02:50]
"So like, have you ever lost 700 grand in a day? Anybody here? I want to resonate. No, nobody's lost 700 grand in a day. Like in like two, three hours. No. Alright. Fuck. Alright, guess I'm the only one."
[03:05]
"So, to explain that feeling to you guys is insane. It was like I think like two years ago, it was like remember when that hurricane that hit Naples that really destroyed everything over there."
[03:20]
"I was trading during that time. I decided to go against my own rules, which is stop-loss, stop-loss, stop-loss, right? Some of you guys are traders. And I just let the trade ride and I was like 500 grand negative, 600 grand negative, 700 grand negative."
[03:40]
"And I was like, 'Fuck.' I went to the office in the middle of a hurricane. This guy like my office like, 'What are you doing? It's in the middle of the fucking hurricane. The palm trees are like moving and shit.' It was like a movie."
[03:55]
"I was like, 'Fuck.' And then I pulled up to the office and I was just like, I'm just like 700 grand negative. The palm trees outside are fucking like I'm like click close."
[04:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lições de uma Perda de $700K"
NÚMEROS: $700,000 perda, 2 anos atrás, 0 grandes perdas desde então
VISUAL: Conceito de aprendizado através da dor, transformação de trader, regras de stop-loss
[04:15]
"But that close that day taught me lessons that that half a million dollar day will never ever fucking teach me. Ask me how much I've lost since then. Have I ever had a big day since then? Loss. No, I learned my lesson that day."
[04:30]
"And that is what you guys got to understand. If you never take risk and you never lose, then you're never going to understand that for you guys to get to that happiness, right?"
[04:45]
"Like there is no happiness without a sad day. There is no sunny days without gloomy days. Look at this beautiful day today. I woke up today. I was like, 'Fuck, this fucking is amazing. I love Miami.'"
[05:00]
"But if Miami didn't have those gloomy days, how would I know what an amazing day looks like? So that day taught me the lessons that I needed to become from two years ago to who I am today."
[05:15]
"And even in the past six years that I've been trading, I've been trading for six years, since 2018, I started trading. I started messing around with Robin Hood. I think everybody here knows what Robin Hood is, right?"
[05:30]
"And I never knew I was going to get to where I am today. I was just looking for another stream of income. Like most of you guys here, how many of you guys knew that you were going to be where you are today?"
[05:45]
"Did anybody here wake up and be like, I'm going to make a 100 mil in marketing. I'm going to make 100 mil e-commerce. I'm going to make 100 million in this that. Literally, anybody? Anybody at all?"
[06:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "O Caminho do Empreendedor = Risco"
NÚMEROS: 6 anos de trading, começou em 2018, ~$100M+ gerados
VISUAL: Jornada de crescimento, conceito de risco calculado, evolução de trader iniciante a expert
[06:05]
"And think about like where how did you get to where you are today? You got to where you are today by taking some type of risk somewhere along the way that got you to where you are today. And that is what trading is about too."
[06:20]
"It's about risk. We're just take risk on an everyday basis. Okay. So, long story short, we've probably made I don't know probably like 100 mil between trading this that right."
[06:35]
"But for you to get to that 100 mark, you first have to go through those 700 grand days losses. Like if you don't risk 100 grand, you cannot make 500 grand. If you don't risk a mill, you're not going to make 5 mil."
[06:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Escala de Risco para Crescimento"
NÚMEROS: Risco $1K → Ganho $1M | Risco $1M → Ganho $100M | Risco $100M → Ganho $1B
VISUAL: Escada de crescimento financeiro, proporção de risco vs recompensa, mentalidade de scaling
[06:55]
"There's a saying, right? You lose a couple thousand to make what? Mill. You lose a couple mill to make couple mill. Couple hundred mil. You lose a couple hundred mil to make a billion dollars. That's what makes you guys entrepreneurs."
[07:15]
"So, everybody here that's like, I'm not a trader. I'm an entrepreneur. Like, I do e-commerce or I do marketing. I do this. I do real estate. I do Airbnb. We're all traders. You're trading something. You're trading some type of skill, right?"
[07:30]
"So, again, I'm not a trader. I just mastered that better than my other attributes, right? Like, I put my trucking company on auto on automation pretty much. I hire a CEO. I hire a marketing. a higher operations manager, had them run everything for me while I focused on trading, which is my passion of what I really wanted to do."
[07:50]
"So, any questions so far?"
[07:52]
"At the beginning, you kind of ask like does anyone know about like trade fun?"
[07:56]
"Yeah. So, we started the prop firm game, right? I'm very transparent. Like honestly, I show my losses every day on Instagram."
[08:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Transparência no Trading"
NÚMEROS: Perdas e ganhos diários mostrados publicamente
VISUAL: Conceito de transparência vs smoke and mirrors, posts de Instagram com resultados reais, credibilidade
[08:10]
"Because a lot of people like listen, you guys got to you guys got to understand something. If I sell you on something like a course and I show you the only wins and you buy a course and it's not how I pictured it, then I look bad, right?"
[08:25]
"It's my name on there. But if I show you there's losses and then wins and you just have to win more than you lose, then you understand the concept. So that's transparency that a lot of people don't have in the trading space."
[08:40]
"So, the prop, the reason why I asked that question is because I heard a lot of you guys are using prop firms to trade. And I'm an owner of a prop firm."
[08:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SEGREDO #1: Como Prop Firms Realmente Funcionam"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Estrutura de prop firm revelada, conceito de casino vs fundos reais, modelo de negócio
[08:55]
"But prop firms don't really trade with you. They trade in the opposite side. The reason for that is very simple. If you go to a casino, right, like how many of you guys is anybody here gambled before or likes to gamble currently?"
[09:10]
"Yeah. Anybody a fucking degenerate? No. Alright. Fuck. It's like a room full of fucking regular people. Damn. Alright. That's boring as fuck. I got I lost like 20 grand in Blackjack the other day."
[09:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Prop Firm = Casino Model"
NÚMEROS: $20,000 perda recente em blackjack
VISUAL: Comparação casino vs prop firm, jogadores pagam uns aos outros, house sempre ganha
[09:30]
"So, like I was like, you know, I was like not my greatest moment. If you go to if you go to Hard Rock, right? Like the guy sitting next to you, he's winning, you're losing. Guess who's paying his winnings, right?"
[09:45]
"And then when it's backwards, guess who's paying your winnings? Okay, proper casino. That's really what it is. There's a lot of prop firms out there saying that they use real funds to fund you with real funds and all that bullshit. It's all bullshit."
[10:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SEGREDO #2: Nenhuma Prop Firm Usa Fundos Reais"
NÚMEROS: 99.99% das prop firms não usam fundos reais
VISUAL: Revelação da mentira, prop firms fora dos EUA, regulamentações, modelo de negócio real
[10:05]
"The only problems that are saying that is because they're registered outside the US. So they're allowed to to lie to you and say that, 'Oh, we use real funds. We back you up with real funds. You guys are trading real funds.' Bullshit."
[10:20]
"I guarantee you 99.99% of no prop firm out there, at least not public firms, right? There is private ones that hire real traders one-on-one. They do an interview process. They hire you. They give you real funds."
[10:35]
"But those traders, those evaluation firms, none of them are actually giving you real funds. What it is is it's a casino, okay? It's a business. It's a business at the end of the day."
[10:50]
"Like everything's a business, you know? You can't get mad at it. You just, okay, that's a good business to have. Fuck let's run it. It's not easy to run. There's a lot of risk management that comes involved in it."
[11:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SEGREDO #3: Taxa de Falha de Traders"
NÚMEROS: 90% dos traders falham, 10% têm sucesso
VISUAL: Estatística de falha, unicórnios traders, payouts de $100K-$200K
[11:10]
"Because obviously you have those good traders that you know I know some I heard some some of you here are fucking hitting prop firms for like 200 grand 100 grand which is insane. So you guys are like the unicorns, right?"
[11:25]
"What percentage of traders do you think fail?"
[11:28]
"99 90 90, right? 99 would be awesome. No, I'm kidding. 90 90, right? So 90% of traders failed, right?"
[11:40]
"So if you have a prop, everybody's buying evaluations, right? And 90% of them are failing. It's not a fucking great business to have. Like you're making 90% profit every single time."
[11:55]
"Like you just keep buying evaluation just keep failing and then you have every now and then a trader that pays it out. 20 30 bands, 40 bands, 200 bands, right? So that's how the prop firm pretty much kind of works."
[12:10]
"Does it hurt to pay that out?"
[12:12]
"Does it hurt? Hell yeah. Fuck. Every time I got to pay out 100 grand, I'm like fuck you know? But who cares? Like I do it, you know, at a certain level like honestly in professionalism you got to be proud of those traders."
[12:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Orgulho em Pagar Traders de Sucesso"
NÚMEROS: Payouts de $100K+
VISUAL: Mentalidade profissional, respeito por traders qualificados, dificuldade de se tornar trader
[12:35]
"Like I'm honestly proud to pay out a trader. Because it takes skill, man. And it takes once you know how hard it is to become. There's a lot of prop firm owners that are never been traders."
[12:50]
"I've been trading for six years and I just started my prop firm six, seven months ago. So, and the reason before before that I never started a prop firm was because I didn't understand it fully and I was still trying to grasp the back end of everything."
[13:10]
"And then I'm like, okay, I understand everything now. But I'm proud when I pay out traders because like man, if you knew how hard it is to become traders like, okay, when you first start trading, 80% of traders fail and they quit within the first month."
[13:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SEGREDO #4: Estatísticas Brutais do Trading"
NÚMEROS: 80% desistem no 1º mês | 20% restantes desistem em 60 dias | Apenas 1-3 pessoas conseguem funding
VISUAL: Funil de desistência, taxa de atrito brutal, raridade de traders bem-sucedidos
[13:35]
"Okay, first month of trading. That means that if you everybody here started trading today, 80% of you would quit the first month. Okay? Then the other 20% would quit within 60 days."
[13:50]
"Okay? And then you have a very very very small amount of you that will actually continue trading long-term. Now, out of these small amount of you that will actually continue longterm, let's say one, two, three."
[14:05]
"How many of you guys how many of those guys do you think actually go out to get funded? One. Say him, right? He goes out to get funded. Does that guarantee that he's going to get a payout though? Not really."
[14:20]
"This is he got funded. But now you still got to continue trading and actually making money. So trading is fucking hard. And I always tell everybody, man, like everybody I'm I don't do courses anymore and stuff like that."
[14:35]
"For the last two years, I haven't really done courses. I haven't really mentored anybody. People are always reaching out to me on Instagram because I still post my profits and my losses. Very transparent. Profits, losses."
[14:50]
"Like yesterday, I lost like 30,000 and I was pissed. I was like, 'Fuck.' But it's part of the game, man. As long as you understand that losses are part of the game, then you're good."
[15:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Perdas São Parte do Jogo"
NÚMEROS: $30,000 perda ontem
VISUAL: Aceitação de perdas, mentalidade de longo prazo, transparência contínua
[15:10]
"So, I'm very transparent in that sense. If you guys get into trading, it's pretty much, like I said, you're going to have losing days. You know what I'm saying? Anybody play blackjack at all, right?"
[15:25]
"So, like, how many hands do you not lose? How many hands do you not win, right? Like, the only difference between trading and blackjack is that blackjack gives you instant gratification. It's at the moment."
[15:40]
"So, you know, if you lost immediately, you're like, 'Fuck, I lost.' But are you mad when you lose? See, like you're kind of like you are mad cuz you lose money, but you kind of like accept the fact that that's just part of the game."
[15:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SEGREDO #5: Gratificação Instantânea vs Realidade"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Comparação blackjack vs trading, aceitação de perdas, problema do ego
[16:00]
"The problem with people and traders, right? Like somebody, for those of you guys that are traders, and even those of you guys are entrepreneurs, not traders, a lot of you guys got to accept that losses in life are part of the game. And that's what gets to your ego."
[16:20]
"A lot of people here, listen, we all have egos. Everybody here thinks you're the best. If you ask me who the best fucking trader is, I'm not going to say anybody else but me. I don't care who you are. I don't care how much proof you show me, right?"
[16:35]
"If you ask Brandon over there, who the fuck is the best fitness trainer in the world? He's gonna say Brandon Carter, he ain't gonna fucking be like that guy over there. You know what I'm saying?"
[16:50]
"If I ask you, what do you do?"
[16:52]
"We work with chiropractors."
[16:54]
"Chiropractor. You work with chiropractors. What do you do with chiropractors?"
[16:57]
"We do lead gen."
[16:59]
"Lead gen. Okay. So, if I ask you, who's the best chiropractor lead gen in the world? Who is it?"
[17:05]
"It's me."
[17:06]
"There you go. Right. So, why the fuck would you say anybody else? So, it's the same fucking right? And that's what you guys got to understand. Like everything in life honestly is always about you."
[17:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Mentalidade: Você É O Melhor"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Conceito de autoconfiança, ego saudável, crença em si mesmo antes de outros
[17:25]
"It's about what you make it about yourself. If you think you're the best, you will become the best eventually. Right now, it takes time for succeeded in other people's eyes. In other people's eyes has it takes time to change. Okay?"
[17:40]
"It's like when you when you go to the gym, right? Like you can't just fucking expect results right away. You're not going to see accept results right away. Other people start seeing results before you see results because other people don't see you every single day."
[17:55]
"You see yourself every single day. You start just you start just to know that you're having results by other people telling you like, 'Yo, you're you're doing good, man. You're losing weight. Yo, you're looking ripped, bro. Same fucking with a business."
[18:10]
"We look for outside gratification to know that we're doing good.' And that fucking that. Like what? Like, bro, believe in yourself before anybody else does. So that by the time that somebody sees you guys actually making money and believes in you, it doesn't even matter."
[18:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Gratificação Externa vs Interna"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Conceito de validação externa, aceitação pessoal, independência mental
[18:35]
"As humans, we look for like gratification and acceptance from other humans. The only person that you need acceptance from and gratification from is yourself. That's why blackjack and trading, same fucking. Blackjack just offers instant gratification."
[18:50]
"You won, oh, I won. You lost. Oh, I lost. And it's like, think about like the world today, bro. Like, we're so used to that instant gratification. That's why some people like they don't want to hit the gym for a year and see results."
[19:05]
"They don't want to like do this to see results. They don't want to start a business, fail, and then see results. You know why? Because of fucking Amazon, bro. Like, yo, Amazon now has same day delivery."
[19:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SEGREDO #6: Problema da Gratificação Instantânea"
NÚMEROS: Entrega no mesmo dia Amazon: pedido 8AM, entrega 12PM
VISUAL: Cultura de gratificação instantânea, Amazon same-day delivery, mentalidade moderna
[19:25]
"Have you guys seen that fucking I ordered some shit the other day, 8:00 a.m. Fucking 12:00 in the afternoon. It was in my house. I'm like, what the fuck is this? That's crazy, right? Is because we're so used to instant gratification."
[19:40]
"You guys got to detach from that fucking. Like, the world ain't like that, okay? Like, instant gratification is not a real thing. You got to detach from that. That's why like a lot of people get into trading and the main reason why they get into trading is because of the money."
[19:55]
"And they think it's a get like get-rich quick scheme. They want to like just jump into trading and start making money like right away and fucking. And it's like like some guys come to me, man, and they're like, 'I want to get into trading.'"
[20:10]
"I'm like I'm like, 'Bro, like you're not even making like 10 grand a month. Like you're not even like like what the fuck are you talking about getting into trading?' 'Yeah, but that's that's that's the reason I want to get into trading, dude. I want to be like you. I want to get like you. I want to I want to make all this money.'"
[20:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SEGREDO #7: Trading É Um 'Rich Man's Sport'"
NÚMEROS: Precisa ganhar $10K/mês antes de começar trading
VISUAL: Aviso crítico, pré-requisitos para trading, rota rápida para falência
[20:35]
"I'm like, 'You don't understand. Trading is a fast route to getting broke. If you don't have money, okay, if you don't already have a business established, if you're not already making money, stay the fuck away from trading, okay?"
[20:50]
"Because that is the reason why so many people give trading a bad name. Why so many people call trading a scam? Because I get somebody, let's say I take a student, right? The guy is making 50K a year, regular, small, working at FedEx."
[21:05]
"Starts getting into trading. Guy has his rent due, pressured, he loses the trade, loses his rent. Fuck. Right. Loses a car payment. Fuck trading is a scam. Didn't work out for me."
[21:20]
"Versus you have entrepreneurs like yourselves, okay? Some guys are making maybe 100 grand a month, half a mill, a mill. You get into trading, you already have business experience. You already understand how businesses work."
[21:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pré-Requisitos Para Trading de Sucesso"

VISUAL: Escada de qualificação, experiência em negócios necessária, entendimento de P&L
[21:40]
"You get into trading and then when you get into trading, you start, okay, I lost 10 grand today, but I made 20. Okay, I'm up 10. You understand what a P&L even is. You know what I'm saying?"
[21:55]
"Most guys don't understand that because they never had any business experience. So like how you going to get into something that you didn't have business experience with? So a lot of guys want to jump into trading to up their income, but they don't understand that trading is a rich game sport."
[22:10]
"And I tell everybody that this is a rich game sport. Who does trading? Who you do trading? How much how much you make a month in trading?"
[22:20]
"500 to a million."
[22:22]
"500 to a million a month in trading. Okay, it's a rich game sport. Now, let me ask you something. Do you really think you can make 500 to a million a month if you were never into business?"
[22:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Exemplo Real: $500K-$1M/mês em Trading"

[22:55]
"Okay, like trading and I tell all my students because like I said, I'm transparent. I don't give a fuck I'm going to make money regardless whether you like me or not. I don't give a fuck."
[23:10]
"So, I'm I rather be transparent with you. So, you know, like, yo, that guy told me the hardcore truth about prop firms, about this, about that, than be like, 'Yeah, he sold smoke and mirrors. I got into trading and I lost my fucking life savings.'"
[23:25]
"No, I told you this fucking is a fucking rich man's sport. You know who gets into trading? Hedge funds, banks. You know who the whales in trading are? Hedge funds, banks, central systems, okay? Big guys."
[23:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quem Realmente Move o Mercado"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Hierarquia do mercado: Hedge funds e bancos no topo, traders individuais são "pequenos peixes"
[23:45]
"Those are the ones that really move the market. You guys, nobody here will ever move the market ever. What you guys are is what what we are. Everybody here, even me, we are little fish."
[24:00]
"We get into the market and we get a piece of the market and we capitalize on that and we get the fuck out and we're happy. And with that, we pay the Ferraris and we pay the Lambos and we pay the McLarens."
[24:15]
"While they are not paying McLarens and Ferrari. You know what they're fucking paying? States. They're playing states. They're paying banks. They're paying like big like they're playing missiles and fucking. Like we're just we're just trying to pay our McLarens and Ferraris and Lambos, bro."
[24:30]
"Like I'm good. But most of you guys are happy with that. Like that's why like some people get into the trading and they're like, 'Oh, I want to trade like a bank.' I'm like, 'What the fuck are you trying to do with that money? Like what what are you trying to do?"
[24:45]
"What do you want to be like a bank for? You want to trade? What? Banks trade for economic reasons. Like banks don't trade for nothing. They trade for economic. They want to shift they want to shift the economy into a certain state for god knows what reason behind the scenes."
[25:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Objetivo Realista: Pagar Seus Luxos"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Ferraris, Lamborghinis, McLarens vs estados e bancos, objetivos realistas vs irrealistas
[25:05]
"You you're not a bank. Like just chill. Just take a little bit from the market. Risk very little, right? Like there's a lot of famous traders out there like recently came out saying like, 'Oh, if you're so good at trading, why risk only 2% 3%. Risk 50% 80% of your account.'"
[25:20]
"The fuck? Because I don't want to fucking lose 50 80% of my account. I explain to you why I'm not going to risk 50% of my account. If I lose 2% of my account today, it's going to take me 4% to get back."
[25:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Matemática do Gerenciamento de Risco"
NÚMEROS: Perde 2% = precisa 4% para recuperar | Perde 50% = precisa 100% para recuperar
VISUAL: Matemática exponencial de recuperação, perigo de riscos altos, tempo 4x maior para recuperar
[25:40]
"Why 4%? Does anybody know the two that you lost and the two that you make up? If I lose 50% of my account now, it's going to take me what to get back, right? Just to get back into the game."
[25:55]
"Now, when you lose 50% of your account, you got to understand something. Your margins are not completely different. Now, you're not a margin of 1 to 10 on 100 grand. You're a margin of 1 to 10 on 50 grand."
[26:10]
"So now it takes you four times longer to get back to break even. And that's what people don't understand. There's a chart I can send it to you guys of how long it will take you to get back after losing so much of the account."
[26:25]
"So perfect risk would be anywhere from 2% and if you're really skilled, you risk about four to 5% for those of you that are traders. Like I risk normally about 4% of my account per trade and then I make an average of about 8 12% profit back."
[26:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Risco Perfeito Por Trade"
NÚMEROS: 2-5% risco por trade | 8-12% retorno médio | Risk-to-reward 1:2 ou 1:3
VISUAL: Estratégia de risco conservadora, retornos consistentes, matemática de lucro sustentável
[26:45]
"Okay. What a lot of people don't understand about this is and a lot of you guys do understand about this. That's why like when I talk to traders like the the mentorship thing about trading is like it it sucks to teach regular people that don't understand business."
[27:00]
"Because like how many of you guys know a business that is brick and mortar? Okay, which means like construction, trucking, even real estate. We'll throw real estate in there that you can make 150% return a year. 200% a year. Anybody, any business, 200% return a year, every year, right?"
[27:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Retornos do Trading vs Negócios Tradicionais"
NÚMEROS: 10%/mês = 120%/ano | 20%/mês = 240%/ano | 150-200% retorno anual impossível em brick & mortar
VISUAL: Comparação de retornos, vantagem do trading, potencial de crescimento acelerado
[27:25]
"Trading gives you that ability. You can make, let's say, at a at a slow steady rate, 10% a month, it's 120% a year. At a faster rate, double that, 240% return a year."
[27:40]
"Trading gives you that ability that other businesses don't give you. But you have to learn how to manage the risk because other businesses have slower risk. You know what I hate about other businesses that you start a business today and you don't find out if it fails a year, two years down the line."
[27:55]
"I hate that fucking. Like I'm a gambler. I want to know right now. And that's what trading gives it to you. Every single day that I trade, I trade from 8 to 10 in the morning. That's it."
[28:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rotina Diária de Trading"
NÚMEROS: 8-10 AM apenas | 1 posição por dia | Segunda a Sexta | 0 trading nos fins de semana
VISUAL: Horário fixo de trabalho, disciplina rígida, trading como negócio estruturado
[28:15]
"That's another misconception. People are like, 'You're behind the screen all day.' I'm like, 'What the fuck am I a nerd? I'm fucking behind a fucking screen all day looking at math.' Like, I want to I treat trading like a business."
[28:30]
"My business in trading is 8 to 10 a.m. I get 8 to 10 a.m. in the in the screen. If I see a setup, I take it. If I lose, I get up from the screen. If I win, I get up from the screen. I only take one position every day, Monday to Friday."
[28:45]
"I don't trade on weekends. I don't trade crypto. I don't trade nothing. All I actually trade is gold versus a dollar because I understand the economics behind it. But you guys can trade anything else you want. You can trade euro. You can trade USD, right?"
[29:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Instrumento de Trade: Gold vs USD"

[29:25]

[29:40]

[29:55]

[31:20]

[31:35]

[31:50]

[36:25]

[36:40]
"Okay so let's say you're You're starting at 3 mil a month and with that 3 mil you're making about or let's say 2 mil and with that 2 mill you're making half a mill. Okay. And now you want to make a mil a month. 2 mil a month."
[36:55]
"What you want to do is raise your account balance. Doesn't mean that you're going to risk all that. What it means is you're going to now your your risk level is going to stay the same percentage wise, but dollar-wise you're risking more and then in profit wise you're risking more."
[37:10]
"Um, and a lot of traders are scared, you know, to raise their account balance because so a lot of people are like, 'Man, how can I make um 10 grand a month?' I'm like, 'Uh, 100 grand in the account.' 100 grand. I'm like, 'Yeah, this ain't gambling, bro.'"
[37:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Matemática de Saldo de Conta"
NÚMEROS: $100-150K conta = $10-30K/mês | $1M conta = $100-200K/mês | 10-20% retorno mensal
VISUAL: Relação direta entre capital e lucro, não é gambling, percentuais realistas
[37:30]
"Like, you know, 10% give or take a month, 15% a month, 20% a month. So, you're going to need about 100 150 grand in your account to make 10, 20, 25 bands, 30 bands a month."
[37:45]
"Um, and a lot of people are scared because of that number. They're like, 'Oh, like let's say somebody wanted to make 100 or 200 grand a month. I'll be like, 'Okay, you need to start at a million dollars account balance.'"
[38:00]
"And people like, 'A million dollars? I gotta risk a million dollars.' And I'm like, 'You don't got to risk a million dollars.' That's your account balance. That is so that your risk-to-reward is in the good a good place where you're not risking 30% of your account to make 30 to make 30%, you know, like it it doesn't make sense to be a one to one."
[38:20]
"So, let's say you start with a million dollars. You start to risk 2%, okay, per trade. And that's it. Now, if you want to make more money, guess what you need to do? You need to inject more money, more capital into the account, making more money."
[38:35]
"The other thing you can do as well is start raising your risk little by little. Okay. Instead of like, I don't know what you're risking per trade right now."
[38:45]
"I go out contracts because I trade profits."
[38:48]

[38:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Aumentar Risco Gradualmente"
NÚMEROS: 2-3% → 5% risco | 1:3 risk-reward | 6% retorno → 15% retorno
VISUAL: Progressão gradual de risco, matemática de multiplicação, max 5% por trade
[39:00]
"Okay. So, let's say you're risking like 2% 3%. You start going to 5% risk and that's going to help you too because now you're one to three is 15% profit, right?"
[39:15]
"So if you're risking 2% a 1 to three is going to get you 6% return. But if you're risking 5% a 1 to 3 is going to get you 15% return. So you can start doing that little by little. Don't go into like okay I'm going to risk 20% of my account."
[39:30]
"Now the max you should ever risk per trade and per position should only be 5%. Overall you should never be risking 10% 20%. Because now you're talking about harder harder to get back returns because you don't know if you're going to lose tomorrow, too."
[39:45]
"No, you're right. I just have to double down."
[39:48]
"Yeah. Yeah. So, so yeah, like I said, it's a it's a it's a rich man's sport. Um, I recommend everybody to get into it. Um, you know, the reason why I always tell everybody is that is because I never knew was where it was going to take me."

[41:35]

[42:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Bots de Trading: Prós e Contras"
NÚMEROS: 6-7 meses de testes | 2-3% risco/dia | 8-10% lucro
VISUAL: Limitações de bots, eventos cisne negro, necessidade de monitoramento 24/7
[42:05]
"Yeah. So, this is the thing about humans, right? What what we talked about earlier with Amazon instant notification. We're looking for the easy easy route to make money. There's no easy route to make money."
[42:20]
"So bots are good short-term. But long term, no bot can see news coming or black swan events. For example, I think I was in um where were we, Mark? I think it was like Mexico or some fucking or I think St. Martin."
[42:35]
"And uh uh fucking uh I think or some fucking I think a missile hit from is Israel attacked Iran something like that just two days ago. Bro, the markets went ballistic. If you would have had a bot on during that event, and this happened like at 5, six p p.m. at night, you would have been fucked right?"
[42:55]
"Unless you had a human actually 24/7 looking at a bot. There's a lot of good bots out there. Um, but very short-term situation like get in, get out, make money. You know, the best thing is obviously manual trading."
[43:10]
"Just get in one once a day, bro. 8 to 10 a.m. Get in. If you lose, you lose. If you win, you win. I guarantee you if you have some type of strategy, just a little bit of edge in the market, you make money."
[43:25]
"But uh we are working on on uh making one right now. Low little low risk. Risk's about 2 3% a day. Uh makes about like 8% 10% profit. So we're working on on that right now. We've been testing it for the past 6 months, 7 months."
[43:40]
"Um I don't like to put my name on anything that is is I don't see actually, you know, like profitable or being tested and stuff like that. That's why it took me so long to even make a prop firm because I wanted to fully understand the prop firm."
[43:55]
"I wanted my rules to be fair for everybody also as well. Like I want people to get paid out. You know what I'm saying? Because because that's what makes you that's what's gonna make your prop firm grow, you know? Like people getting paid out, not people not getting paid out cuz then you're going to have a bunch of bad reviews and nobody's going to go to your prop firm."
[44:15]

[46:10]

[46:25]

[47:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Estratégia de Aprendizado"

[48:05]
"Any other questions that we got here before we wrap it up? You trade overnight?"
[48:10]
"Overnight? Uh, very very rarely. Like one out of a 100 trades I I trade overnight. Uh, if I do, obviously, I have my stop loss set and then I wake up, you know, some trades I wake up in profit because I never set up take profit. I always just set a stop-loss."
[48:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Trading Overnight: Estratégia"
NÚMEROS: 1 em 100 trades overnight
VISUAL: Sempre stop-loss, nunca take-profit, maximizar ganhos potenciais
[48:35]
"Uh, because, for example, like some trades I trade overnight, I put a stop-loss, I let it ride, I wake up like fucking hella money in profit. I'm like, fucking. And I close it. Um, because if you set a a take-profit, you kind of limit your gains. You know what I'm saying?"
[48:50]
"At least with my stop loss, I know I'm going to lose that amount of money and I'm I'm okay with that, you know."
[48:55]
"What's your average duration trade? 10 minutes?"
[49:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Duração e Timeframes"
NÚMEROS: 15-30 min execução | 4H, Daily, Weekly, Monthly charts | 1 hora média por trade
VISUAL: Estrutura de timeframes, análise multi-temporal, execução rápida
[49:05]
"No. So I trade on 15 minute, 30 minute execution and then I chart on 4 hour, daily, weekly, and monthly. So when I execute, I'm on the 15 30 minute chart. So normally about an hour, hour and a half in a trade."
[49:20]
"Uh some trades, if it's a news event, I'm in it for 15 minutes and I'm out. But normally about an hour. Like let's say I get in a trade about like 9:00 a.m. I usually be out by 10, you know, out of the trade."
[49:35]
"So when you really look at how much money you're making, because you got to understand like let's say you make 90 grand trading in a day, you really got to break it down to how much money how much time did I really spend behind a computer?"
[49:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "ROI de Tempo Real"
NÚMEROS: $90K lucro em 1 hora de trabalho | 100% lucro líquido
VISUAL: ROI insano, sem ad spend, sem chefe, sem 9-5, puro lucro
[49:55]
"Like I just spent an hour and I just made 90 grand. Like that's that's a crazy ROI, you know, and it's net. Like you don't got to answer to no ad spend, no boss, no nothing. It ain't a nine-to-5, you know. So, it's like it's all pure profit."
[50:10]
"So, that's why I'm telling you guys like if you guys are already making money, I highly recommend it. Uh, you know, like if you're everybody here's at a certain level where you're already making, you know, nobody's here like it's like making $2,000 a week or something like that."
[50:25]
"You know what I'm saying? Like everybody's making substantial amount of money. So, highly recommend it so you guys can just scale that money to the next level. And just understand it's a process. You know what I'm saying?"
[50:40]
"Is it's going to be it's going to take you a couple months to get it, but once you do get it, you're going to recover whatever you lost plus times 10 that. So,"
[50:50]
"How much time goes into your credit for your friends? So, the the research, the news, you said spent two hours on the computer."
[50:58]
"Yeah, two hours."
[51:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Preparação e Pesquisa Diária"
NÚMEROS: 2 horas/dia total | Calendário econômico verificado às 8AM
VISUAL: Rotina minimalista, calendário de notícias, eventos principais (CPI, juros)
[51:05]
"Bro, honestly, I just get in. I just get in right there. 8 a.m. I open um my effects book. It uh shows me the economic calendar. So, it shows me what news are about to come out."
[51:20]
"So I can see when news are about to come out. If there's anything that's about to come out, um I I try to avoid it or if it's something that I'm very familiar with, like for example, the uh job reports, I'm familiar with the job reports. So, I just wing it. I'm like, 'All right, is it going to buy or sell?'"
[51:35]
"And then I just place my one thing about trading news. If you guys are traders, use pending orders. Never use market execution. That's a trick. You guys are trading news. Use pending orders because the market's going to wick."
[51:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "DICA: Pending Orders para Notícias"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Técnica de pending orders, evitar wicks, stop-loss automático, execução inteligente
[51:55]
"If you use a pending order with a stop-loss, it's less likely that you're going to get like stopped out quickly versus it's going to grab your pending order. You have your stop loss already set. It's going to either continue or it's going to hit your stop loss."
[52:10]
"So, it helps me out a lot with uh trading news. But um one thing I do prepare for is uh major news events, which is called red folders. So, for example, you're going to have like uh CPI data um coming out, which is basically like interest rates."
[52:25]
"Interest rates affect gold and the US dollar a lot because I'm trading gold versus the dollar. So, if that's going to come out, I do prepare for before I start looking for research and stuff like that, like maybe two days prior if I'm going to trade it."
[52:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Estado Atual: Gold em All-Time High"
NÚMEROS: Gold em máxima histórica
VISUAL: Estratégia de espera, deixar mercado corrigir, retomar quando voltar ao normal
[52:45]
"These days, I I'm honestly avoiding news. Gold is at all-time high right now. So, I'm like taking vacations. I'm letting that thing come back down. When it comes back down, then I start kind of like doing my routine of everyday trading right now."
[53:00]
"So, yeah, doesn't take too lot too much preparation, bro. Trading doesn't take a lot of your time. A lot of people don't get into trading because they think it's going to take away from their business. Trust me, you'll be fine."
[53:15]
"Like hour, two hours a day dedication, you you're fine. In the beginning, because you're learning, you're going to have to dedicate time to learning like everything in life. But the first two, three months after that, you're you're set."
[53:30]
"After that, you just got to be an hour, two hours in the computer and fucking making money, bro. So, any other questions? Anybody questions? No. Good. All right, guys. I'm going wrap it up here. Appreciate you, Jeremy."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
6 anos de experiência em trading (desde 2018)
~$100M+ gerados entre trading e negócios
Maior ganho em um dia: $500K
Maior perda em um dia: $700K (há 2 anos)
Empresa de caminhões: 150 trucks
Prop firm iniciada há 6-7 meses
90% dos traders falham
80% desistem no primeiro mês
20% restantes desistem em 60 dias
Perdas recentes: $30K, $28K, $24K (3 dias consecutivos)
Ganhos semana anterior: $200K (2 trades)
Risco por trade: 4% do saldo
Retorno médio: 8-12% por trade
Horário de trading: 8-10 AM apenas
1 posição por dia, segunda a sexta
Primeiro trade: $30 lucro (American Airlines)
Evolução: $100K conta → $108K (+$8K em minutos)
Exemplo crypto: $10K → $180K em 6 dias (Elon Coin)
Retorno anual: 120-240% possível
Saldo necessário para $10-30K/mês: $100-150K
Saldo necessário para $100-200K/mês: $1M
Bot em desenvolvimento: 2-3% risco, 8-10% lucro diário
6-7 meses de testes de bot
Perda recente em blackjack: $20K
99.99% das prop firms não usam fundos reais
Payouts negados principalmente por: bots e múltiplos IPs
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Instrumento principal: Gold vs USD (entende a economia por trás)
Timeframes de análise: 4H, Daily, Weekly, Monthly charts
Timeframes de execução: 15-30 minutos
Duração média de trade: 1 hora
Gerenciamento de risco: 2-5% máximo por trade
Stop-loss obrigatório: Sempre configurado
Take-profit: Nunca usa (para maximizar ganhos)
Pending orders: Usa para trading de notícias
Eventos principais: CPI data, interest rates, job reports
Preparação: 2 dias antes de red folder events
Calendário econômico: Verificado diariamente às 8 AM
Estado atual: Evitando trades (Gold em all-time high)
ESTRUTURA:
Prop firms funcionam como cassinos: Traders pagam uns aos outros
Nenhuma prop firm pública usa fundos reais: 99.99% são modelo casino
Modelo de negócio: 90% falham (lucro da casa), 10% ganham payouts
Negação de payouts: Regras ocultas, múltiplos IPs, uso de bots
Regras da prop firm dele: Transparentes, pagam a maioria
Taxa de sucesso brutal: 80% primeiro mês, 20% em 60 dias, apenas 1-3 conseguem funding
Mentoria: Parou há 2 anos, não vende mais cursos
Transparência: Mostra perdas e ganhos diários no Instagram
High ticket vs low ticket: $100 curso vs $100K mentoria = comprometimento diferente
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
ROI de tempo: $90K em 1 hora = ROI insano
Lucro líquido: 100% (sem ad spend, sem chefe, sem 9-5)
Retornos anuais: 120% (10%/mês) a 240% (20%/mês)
Comparação: Nenhum negócio brick & mortar faz 150-200% ao ano
Matemática de recuperação: Perde 2% = precisa 4% para recuperar | Perde 50% = precisa 100%
Risk-to-reward: 1:2 ou 1:3 (risco 4%, ganha 8-12%)
Scaling: Aumentar saldo da conta, não % de risco
Capital inicial recomendado: $100-150K para $10-30K/mês
Para $100-200K/mês: Precisa $1M de capital
Custo de "aprender grátis": 2 anos + centenas de milhares perdidos
Custo de mentor: $5-10K (economiza anos e dinheiro)
FILOSOFIA E MENTALIDADE:
Trading = Rich man's sport: Precisa já estar ganhando dinheiro
Não é get-rich-quick: É para quem já tem negócio estabelecido
Perdas são parte do jogo: Aceitar como no blackjack
Lição da perda de $700K: Ensinou mais que ganho de $500K
Gratificação instantânea: Problema da geração Amazon (same-day delivery)
Ego saudável: "Sou o melhor" = mentalidade necessária
Aceitação interna vs externa: Não precisa validação de outros
Consistência vs jumping: Ficar com um mentor, não pular
Responsabilidade pessoal: Não é o mentor, é você
Risco é necessário: Não arrisca $100K, não ganha $500K
Feedback imediato: Vantagem sobre negócios tradicionais (1-2 anos)
Transparência total: Mostra perdas e ganhos (credibilidade)
CONSELHOS PRÁTICOS:
Escolha uma categoria: Crypto, Stocks, Futures ou Forex
YouTube primeiro: Para entender conceitos básicos (grátis)
Depois pague mentor: Economiza 2 anos e centenas de milhares
Pré-requisito: Já estar ganhando $10K+/mês em outro negócio
Evite se: Não tem dinheiro, está sob pressão financeira
Tempo necessário: 1-2 horas/dia após primeiros 2-3 meses
Bots: Testar com max $100K, nunca $1-2M
Pending orders: Usar para trading de notícias
Max risco: 5% por trade, nunca mais
Scaling: Aumentar capital, não % de risco
DURAÇÃO: ~53 minutos FORMATO: Palestra/Q&A em evento presencial (Miami)
